hero.title: Sell your USDT for Colombian pesos — safe, legal, fast
hero.subtitle: Receive cash or bank transfer. Full KYC process, SARLAFT compliance. Physical locations in Medellín and Envigado.

howItWorks.title: How does it work?
howItWorks.step1Desc: Message us on WhatsApp with the amount of USDT you want to sell and we'll give you today's rate instantly.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Complete our identity and source-of-funds verification. Required by law for all clients — passport accepted for tourists.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Visit one of our physical locations or receive a bank transfer. We deliver COP in cash or to your Colombian bank account.

whoWeServe.title: Who do we serve?
whoWeServe.touristsDesc: Arrived in Medellín with USDT and need pesos to get around? No Colombian bank account needed. We accept passports.
whoWeServe.localsDesc: Traders, freelancers, or crypto remittance recipients who want to convert USDT or BTC to pesos with legal backing and regulatory compliance.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Information about crypto, compliance and the Colombian market

compliance.title: Compliance & security
compliance.subtitle: We operate under the highest Colombian regulatory compliance standards
compliance.kycDesc: All our clients go through a mandatory Know Your Customer (KYC) process including official ID verification and source-of-funds validation.
compliance.sarlaftDesc: We have an implemented anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism financing risk management system (SARLAFT), in compliance with current Colombian regulations.
compliance.legalDesc: BitWave S.A.S. is a legally incorporated technology solutions company in Colombia, operating in accordance with applicable current regulations.
compliance.disclaimer: Legal notice: BitWave is not an entity supervised by the Superintendencia Financiera de Colombia. Crypto-asset exchange is not covered by Fogafin deposit insurance. Crypto-assets are not legal tender in Colombia. Operations are conducted under applicable current Colombian regulations.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: What documents do I need to exchange?
faq.a1: For Colombian citizens: national ID (cédula). For foreigners and tourists: valid passport. In all cases we apply full KYC and source-of-funds verification.
faq.q2: What are the minimum and maximum amounts?
faq.a2: The minimum is 100 USDT. For operations above certain amounts, additional verification applies per our SARLAFT compliance policy. Contact us for large-volume operations.
faq.q3: How is the exchange rate calculated?
faq.a3: The rate is based on the USDT market price at the time of the transaction, with an operational spread. We don't guarantee fixed rates — contact us on WhatsApp for the current quote.
faq.q4: How long does the operation take?
faq.a4: Once KYC is complete and USDT are received in our wallet, cash delivery at our physical locations is immediate. Bank transfers are processed on the same business day.
faq.q5: Is it legal to exchange USDT in Colombia?
faq.a5: Yes. Crypto-asset exchange is not prohibited in Colombia. BitWave operates as a legally incorporated company, fulfilling anti-money laundering obligations and other applicable regulations.
faq.q6: I'm a tourist without a Colombian ID — can you help me?
faq.a6: Yes. We serve international tourists with a valid passport. It is the official document recognized in our KYC process for foreign clients.
faq.q7: Which USDT networks do you accept?
faq.a7: We accept USDT on TRC20 (Tron) and ERC20 (Ethereum) networks. Check the available network at the time of your transaction. Unverified networks are not accepted.
faq.q8: Do I receive pesos in cash or bank transfer?
faq.a8: Both options are available. At our physical locations we deliver cash pesos instantly. We can also make a bank transfer to a Colombian account (same business day).

contact.title: Contact us
contact.subtitle: Available on WhatsApp for quotes and inquiries

footer.disclaimer: BitWave is not supervised by the Superintendencia Financiera de Colombia. Crypto-assets are not legal tender.
